message:
fix(resource): batch observer notifications outside the lock to prevent contention

ResourceTracker::try_acquire() and ResourceGuard::Drop held the inner
mutex while iterating observers and calling on_pressure_change(),
on_limit_approached(), and on_limit_exceeded(). If an observer needed
any lock (even indirectly through logging or metrics), this created
contention or potential deadlock.

Introduces NotificationBatch: a struct that collects which notifications
need to fire while the lock is held, then dispatches them after the lock
is dropped. The observers vector now stores Arc<dyn ResourceObserver>
(was Box<dyn ResourceObserver>) so cloning the observer list into the
batch is cheap.

Refactored functions:
- notify_pressure() -> prepare_pressure_notifications(): returns batch
- notify_limit_exceeded() -> prepare_limit_exceeded(): returns batch
- notify_limit_approached() inlined into prepare_pressure_notifications()

All three call sites (try_acquire success, try_acquire failure,
ResourceGuard::drop) now follow the pattern:
  let batch = { lock(); compute; unlock; };
  batch.dispatch();

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
